featureName: BUG-021-004: relationship-cooling heuristic was untested inline SQL and deviates from the spec's ≥1/week shorthand
supersededNote: Owner directive (no const limits; LLM decides per situation). BUG-021-005 replaces the extracted constants + the TestRelationshipCoolingHeuristic_MatchesDocumentedContract lock test with an LLM-driven relationship_cooling_evaluate scenario. The constants/builder/lock test were removed by BUG-021-005; this packet remains as the historical record of the interim constant-extraction step.

executionHistory.summary: Sweep round 9 (regression lens) of spec 021 found ProduceRelationshipCoolingAlerts encodes its heuristic as inline SQL magic numbers with NO test, and the threshold (>=4 distinct interactions in the prior 90-day window) is looser than the spec R-021-005/UC-005 shorthand 'previously >= 1/week'. Two problems: untested heuristic (silent drift risk) + spec/code disagreement (a product decision).

Single bugfix-fastlane scope (engineering concern): extract the heuristic into documented constants + a testable builder (identical SQL, no behavior change) and add a lock test. Surface the threshold-direction disagreement as DI-021-004 (owner decision) rather than silently changing behavior or the parent spec. SCN-021-COOLING-01 authored.

internal/intelligence/alert_producers.go: added named constants (coolingSilenceMinDays=30, coolingPriorWindowStartDays=180, coolingPriorWindowEndDays=90, coolingMinPriorInteractions=4, coolingDedupWindowDays=30, coolingMaxAlertsPerRun=10) + relationshipCoolingAlertQuery() builder (fmt.Sprintf over integer constants only). ProduceRelationshipCoolingAlerts calls the builder; the produced SQL is byte-for-byte the prior inline literal. The comment surfaces the >=1/week deviation.

Added TestRelationshipCoolingHeuristic_MatchesDocumentedContract: asserts the produced query embeds the documented threshold fragments (independently hardcoded as the contract) and that the constants back them. PASS on the shipped heuristic; adversarial drift (set coolingMinPriorInteractions=13) makes it FAIL (fragment + constant assertions). go build green.
Full internal/intelligence package green (ok 0.047s) with the lock test; existing producer/helper tests unchanged (no behavior change since the SQL is identical).
Constants + one builder function; no new abstractions; the builder makes the heuristic testable without a live DB.
fmt.Sprintf over integer constants only (no user input, no injection); deterministic query; no perf change (identical SQL).
The SQL is built from integer constants only; no user input reaches the format string. No secret/auth surface. No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; go build + full intelligence package green. Evidence in report.md.

Diff confined to alert_producers.go + alert_producers_test.go; no migration (SQL identical); no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 021 requirement text deliberately untouched (DI-021-004 surfaced, not applied).
alert_producers.go comment documents the heuristic contract + the surfaced >=1/week deviation + the owner-decision pointer (DI-021-004).

scopeProgress.title: Make the cooling heuristic explicit and test-guarded

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane refactor + lock test; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: The relationship-cooling heuristic was inline SQL magic numbers with no test, so a threshold change would drift silently.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Extracted the heuristic into named, documented constants + a testable relationshipCoolingAlertQuery() builder (identical SQL, no behavior change); added TestRelationshipCoolingHeuristic_MatchesDocumentedContract which fails on a threshold drift.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test Evidence (PASS + adversarial drift FAIL); internal/intelligence/alert_producers.go constants + builder; full intelligence package ok.

discoveredIssues.summary: The shipped cooling threshold (>=4 distinct interactions in the prior 90-day window, ~1 per 22 days) is looser than spec 021 R-021-005/UC-005 shorthand 'previously >= 1/week'. Reconciling the two changes the user's live cooling-alert frequency (a product decision). Option A: keep the shipped value, refresh the spec wording to match. Option B: tighten coolingMinPriorInteractions toward a weekly cadence. Both are a one-line change to the constant + the test fragment. This fix locked the SHIPPED behavior and did NOT decide the direction or edit the parent spec requirement text.
discoveredIssues.owner: operator (product decision); then specs/021-intelligence-delivery spec-owner for the wording refresh
